title: rosa hirta var. subglabra
intro: Rosa hirta var. subglabra, also known as the hairy-leaved rose or Rosa subglabra, is a plant species within the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to Europe. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rosa hirta var. subglabra include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
